variants: Scuffed boards.
A bent nail, half-buried.
Sawdust, old.
A faint chalk line.
A coin-shaped stain.
Boot prints, going somewhere.
Splinters, swept aside.
A pressed flower, dried flat.
Two planks, unevenly joined.
A dark stain that isn't paint.
Grit underfoot.
A scrape mark, deep.
Chalk numbers, half-erased.
Something sticky, origin unclear.
A square of sunlight, moving slowly.

Y.default: A tree. Its leaves are still.
variants: An old oak. Initials carved into it, worn too smooth to read.
A young birch, its bark peeling in long curls.
A dead pine. A crow watches from the highest branch.
An apple tree, fruitless this season.
A tree with a small shrine at its base. The offerings are long rotten.
A tree with a frayed rope swing. No one has used it in years.
A maple. Sap drips into a tin can someone left.
A tree split by lightning, still standing.
A pine sapling, planted recently by someone optimistic.
A gnarled tree growing at an unsettling angle.
A tree whose roots have cracked the path nearby.
An elm, tall and indifferent.
A fig tree, dry.
A tree with a nail in it and nothing else.
A willow. It makes the light softer here.

:.default: A dirt path, worn by foot traffic.
variants: The path here is packed hard.
Footprints going in both directions.
A well-trodden route. Someone has paced it many times.
The path narrows slightly here.
Stones pressed into the dirt to keep it dry.
The path bends here, for no visible reason.
A worn groove where a cart wheel has passed, repeatedly.
Worn flat by years of the same footsteps.
The ground is damp here. The path holds water.
A muddy stretch. The prints here overlap.
The outer wall. Solid.
A boundary. Beyond it, the wider world.
Rough stone. Cold to the touch.
The wall here is older than the rest.
A crack runs top to bottom. No concern, probably.
Lichen has taken hold. Green in the mortar.
Someone has scratched a mark here. It isn't decorative.
The wall holds the cold long after dawn.
Still water. Something moves just below the surface.
The pond catches the light.
Cold and dark. You can see the bottom, mostly.
A dragonfly lands briefly, then vanishes.
A small yellow flower. It doesn't know how hard things are.
A red wildflower growing where it wasn't planted.
Purple flowers in a loose cluster. They smell faintly of something.
Tall grass, rustling.
The grass is over your knees here.
Something small moved through here recently.
Grass gone to seed.

&.default: A fallen log, moss-covered. It's been here longer than the path.

+.default: A corner of the building frame.

RM.door: The door to the raw materials shed stands open. Inside, stacked bundles of unprocessed material.
RM.wall: The shed wall smells faintly of resin and old work. The boards are weathered but solid.
RM.label: The raw materials shed. You buy stock here each morning before the price adjusts.

WB.door: The workbench entrance. The door is low — you have to duck.
WB.wall: The workbench walls are worn smooth where hands have steadied against them over many years.
WB.label: Your workbench. One widget at a time. It's slow, but it's yours.

MT.door: The market entrance. A bell above the door rings when it's open.
MT.wall: The market facade. A price list is nailed next to the door. The numbers have been crossed out and rewritten many times.
MT.label: The market. The buyers come at dawn and leave at dusk. What they want changes.

OF.door: The Office door. Most of what's inside is locked down.
OF.wall: The Office frame, plain gray. It's waiting for something.
OF.label: The Office. A skill tree you haven't unlocked yet. The corkboard inside is full of crossed-out plans.

LF.wall: A reinforced wall. Built to withstand something significant.
LF.door: The entrance to the Launch Facility. The air smells of fuel and ambition.
LF.body: The rocket. It has been here longer than you realized. It was always going to end this way.
LF.label: The Launch Facility. Whatever this was built for, it is almost ready.

ST.wall: A warehouse. The padlock on the door is heavy and new. Someone was expecting to use it.
ST.label: Storage. Enough room for a much larger operation than the one you currently run.

BK.wall: A stone building, solid. The lights are off and have been for some time.
BK.label: The bank. Your financial history is on file here. They know more about you than you'd like.

TR.wall: A terminal of some kind. The screen is dark. The keyboard has too many keys.
TR.label: Terminal. You don't know what this is yet.

GS.wall: The shop front. A few items are visible through the window.
GS.door: The General Store entrance. A small bell above the door is silent for now.
GS.label: The General Store. It seems to sell clothing of some kind.

NP.wall: The press building. You can hear machinery inside.
NP.door: The Newspaper office entrance. The smell of ink is strong.
NP.label: The Daily Widget. Est. recently. Circulation: unknown.

tiles.28,6: A loose nail sticks up at an odd angle. Someone has bent it down partway, then given up.
tiles.52,20: The ground here is worn in a circle, as if something heavy was once dragged in a loop. Nobody remembers what.
tiles.7,36: A child's drawing scratched into the wood with a tool. A house, a tree, a stick figure. The figure is waving.
tiles.72,5: A single boot print in dried mud, pointing away from everything. Size eleven, maybe twelve.
tiles.45,38: Three words written in chalk on the ground, faded almost to nothing. You can make out the first: 'Remember'.

locked.launch_facility: A large structure in the corner, shrouded in canvas. Something tall is underneath. The canvas smells of grease and oxidizer.
locked.storage: A warehouse you don't have keys for yet. The padlock is new. Someone anticipated filling this.
locked.bank: A stone building. The hours are posted on the door but the times have been scratched out.
locked.terminal: A terminal behind a locked panel. The screen is dark. You press a key. Nothing. You try again. Still nothing.
locked.office_nodes: A corkboard inside the Office, behind a door that won't open. The notes are in your own handwriting, somehow.
locked.general_store: A small shop, shuttered. A sign in the window reads: 'OPENING SOON.' The display inside is covered with a cloth.
locked.newspaper: A small print shop, dark. A sign reads: 'THE DAILY WIDGET — COMING SOON.' Ink stains on the doorstep.
